title: quintinia resinosa
intro: Quintinia resinosa, also known as the New Zealand fivefinger or tītoki, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive five-fingered leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine, where the resin is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quintinia resinosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The resin can be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource in traditional and modern medicine.
